channel.title: Documentation on Yakkl
channel.description: Recent content in Documentation on Yakkl

item.title: About Organizations
item.description: Organizations are available from our starter plan. Organizations enable you to manage groups of users across multiple sites. Teams or individuals within an organization can have their access levels applied on a per-site basis. Create an Organization You can create an organization on your dashboard by clicking on Create Organization in the left sidebar. Once you have filled in your information and named the organization, you can proceed by clicking Create.

item.title: Product Tour
item.description: Overview This tour will be a walk through of Yakkl showing you how to get the most out of a Secure Team Collaboration platform. Getting Started Sign up for a Yakkl account. Something cool from Yakkl here :) Congrats, you’re ready to yakkl! Yakkl is Open Sourced and hosted on (GitHub. If you or your team wants to run Yakkl on your own systems then please feel free to clone the repo.

item.title: Introduction
item.description: Introduction Yakkl.com is a Secure Team Collaboration platform that allows you and your team to stay caught up on topics of discussions in a simple and organized manner without creating “Channel Sprawl”. If you’re yakkling with the person next to you or multiple timezones away, Yakkl keeps all of your communications organized into Topics so you can pickup where you left off. Sign up for free today. Advantages Choosing to build a static site and use a Git-backed CMS comes with a multitude of advantages over the traditional Monolithic CMS:

item.title: Sidebar Configuration
item.description: You can define which areas (folders) of your site contain editable content files via the sections parameter in your .yakkl/settings.yml configuration file. A content section in Yakkl is primarily defined by the path to the folder containing content files, and a glob to match against the contents of that folder. Options Key Description Type Default type Type of content directory,document,heading,jekyll-pages,jekyll-posts directory label Text to display in Yakkl menu String N/A path Path to folder from repository root (only applies to directory type) String N/A match Glob of files to match inside of path (only applies to directory type) String (Fileglob) **/* exclude Glob pattern of files to exclude (only applies to directory type) String (Fileglob) None create Restrictions on creating new content items all, documents, none all templates Control Which Front Matter Templates can be used in section Array All templates new_doc_ext File extension for new documents created in this section String Value of New File Format in Yakkl Settings read_only Prevent content from being edited (only applies to document type) Boolean false Sidebar Order Sections will appear in the sidebar in the same order they are defined.

item.title: Overview
item.description: Logo The logo setting allows you to customize the branding of Yakkl CMS by allowing you to replace the Yakkl logo in various touchpoints in the CMS. Site Name Site name allows you to customize the display name of your site in the Yakkl UI. This will update the display in the dashboard, in notifications, and various touchpoints. URL The URL setting allows you to provide the URL to your production website, which allows editors to open the production website from the sidebar.

item.title: Pro Plans
item.description: Organizations on the Pro Plan have access to certain enterprise features, please contact us if you would like to upgrade your plan. Subdomains Users within a pro organization will access the app through *organization-subdomain*.yakkl.com instead of app.yakkl.com. A separate user account will be created for each of a users subdomains. For example: If I am an active Yakkl.com user, and I am invited to join Pro Org A, a separate user account will be created scoped to Pro Org A, which I can access through pro-org-a.

item.title: Front Matter Templates
item.description: DisclaimerThis guide assumes you already have an understanding of Front Matter and how it works with Yakkl. If this isn’t the case, you can read up on it in the Front Matter doc. What are Front Matter Templates? Front Matter Templates (FMTs) enable developers to fully customize the interface of the Markdown and HTML editors. You can think of them as the content model for your front matter.

item.title: Single Sign-on
item.description: SAML SSO Foresty supports SAML as a single sign-on solution. Multiple identity providers can be set up and enabled concurrently, allowing for easy certificate rotation. Supported Identity Providers OneLogin Okta Auth0 Ping Identity Azure Active Directory ADFS Other identity providers can be configured, however official setup guides are not yet available. Attributes Email FirstName LastName Teams (for Cloud Enterprise, as a list of TeamIDs) Memberships (for Private Enterprise, in the format of OrganizationID/TeamID) Username, Name, and MemberOf are not used by Yakkl but may still be sent.

item.title: Fields
item.description: Text Single line text input. Displayed for Front Matter values that are short strings. For example, a page title. --- title: "My New Page Title!" --- See detailed documentation Textarea Multi-line text input. Displayed for Front Matter values that are long strings. For example, a page description. --- description: "Write an awesome description for your new site here. It will appear in your document head meta (for Google search results) and in your RSS feed's site description.

item.title: Config Files
item.description: Yakkl stores the settings and configuration of the CMS for each site in a .yakkl/ folder in your site’s source code. This allows developers to create default configurations that can be shared between multiple sites, and to deliver source code with Yakkl CMS pre-configured. When importing a new site, a .yakkl/ folder will be added to your site’s source. Any changes made to your CMS’ configuration will be committed to your site’s source in this folder.

item.title: Collaborators & Teams
item.description: Yakkl allows you to define how you work as teams or with collaborators. Teams are available from our starter plan. On personal sites Sites that are not part of an organization are personal sites. On those sites you can add up to 3 collaborators. Each collaborator can be an admin, developer or editor. Upgrade to our starter plan if you need more collaborators. Managing Collaborators Admins and organization owners can manage site collaborators.

item.title: Import Button
item.description: The import button lets users create their own copy of your project and import it into Yakkl in one fell swoop. If you have a static site project — like a theme or starter site — that is optimized for use with Yakkl and you want to share it, adding an import button to your readme or website will make it easy for users to get started using your project with Yakkl.

item.title: Beta Testers
item.description: From time to time, we will invite some of our users to test new additions to the platform. How to Become a Beta Tester If you are interested in participating in beta testing, join our Slack community. We will announce beta features there, and coordinate feeback in a dedicated channel for the participants of the beta. Documentation For Beta Features We are using LaunchDarkly to control feature access for users of the app.

item.title: Date Field
item.description: A date and time picker. Options General Label — the human-friendly label shown above the input field in the editor. Name — the key stored in your content’s front matter, used to access it in your templates. Description — a human friendly description of what the field does and/or instructions for your editors. Hidden — hides the field in the editor, but allows developers to set default values or maintain the field for legacy purposes.

item.title: Snippets
item.description: Snippets in Yakkl are pre-defined chunks of text that can be inserted into your content. You can put anything in a snippet that you might normally insert into your content, but they are especially useful in conjunction with shortcodes in Hugo or certain Liquid tags in Jekyll. Creating Snippets To create a snippet, add a .snippet file to the .yakkl/snippets directory in your repository. This file should contain the content that you wish to be inserted when your snippet is applied in the editor.

item.title: Color Picker
item.description: The color picker provides an interface for selecting a color and storing the RGB or hex value. Options General Label — the human-friendly label shown above the input field in the editor. Name — the key stored in your content’s front matter, used to access it in your templates. Description — a human friendly description of what the field does and/or instructions for your editors. Hidden — hides the field in the editor, but allows developers to set default values or maintain the field for legacy purposes.

item.title: Blocks
item.description: The Blocks field type is a repeatable array of field groups. Unlike the Repeatable Field Group, however, the field groups within a Blocks field do not all have to be the same. When a user adds a block, they first select which type of block they wish to add. Blocks can be reordered in the UI by dragging and dropping. Options General Label — the human-friendly label shown above the input field in the editor Name — the key stored in your content’s front matter, used to access it in your templates Description — a human-friendly description of what the field does and/or instructions for your editors Hidden — hides the field in the editor; but allows developers to set default values or maintain the field for legacy purposes.

item.title: Include Template
item.description: The Include Template field type allows you include fields that you have already specified in another front matter template. This can be useful for organizing your front matter and consolidating frequently-used fields: for example, including an seo front matter template that contains description, keyword, and thumbnail data. Options General Label — the human-friendly label shown above the input field in the editor Name — this field is not used for this field type Description — a human-friendly description of what the field does and/or instructions for your editors Hidden — hides the filed in the editor; but allows developers to set default values or maintain the field for legacy purposes.

item.title: Field Group
item.description: Options General Label – the human-friendly label shown above the input field in the editor. Name – the key stored in your content’s front matter, used to access it in your templates. Description – a human friendly description of what the field does and/or instructions for your editors. Hidden – hides the field in the editor, but allows developers to set default values or maintain the field for legacy purposes.

item.title: File Upload Field
item.description: Options General Label — the human-friendly label shown above the input field in the editor. Name — the key stored in your content’s front matter, used to access it in your templates. Description — a human friendly description of what the field does and/or instructions for your editors. Hidden — hides the field in the editor, but allows developers to set default values or maintain the field for legacy purposes.

item.title: Multi-File Upload Field
item.description: Options General Label — the human-friendly label shown above the input field in the editor. Name — the key stored in your content’s front matter, used to access it in your templates. Description — a human friendly description of what the field does and/or instructions for your editors. Hidden — hides the field in the editor, but allows developers to set default values or maintain the field for legacy purposes.

item.title: Number Field
item.description: Text input that only accepts numeric values. Options General Label — the human-friendly label shown above the input field in the editor. Name — the key stored in your content’s front matter, used to access it in your templates. Description — a human friendly description of what the field does and/or instructions for your editors. Hidden — hides the field in the editor, but allows developers to set default values or maintain the field for legacy purposes.

item.title: Repeatable Field Group
item.description: A repeatable array of fields. Options General Label — the human-friendly label shown above the input field in the editor. Name — the key stored in your content’s front matter, used to access it in your templates. Description — a human friendly description of what the field does and/or instructions for your editors. Hidden — hides the field in the editor, but allows developers to set default values or maintain the field for legacy purposes.

item.title: Select Field
item.description: Options General Label — the human-friendly label shown above the input field in the editor. Name — the key stored in your content’s front matter, used to access it in your templates. Description — a human friendly description of what the field does and/or instructions for your editors. Hidden — hides the field in the editor, but allows developers to set default values or maintain the field for legacy purposes.

item.title: Sortable List Field
item.description: A list of values with drag & drop reordering. Options General Label — the human-friendly label shown above the input field in the editor. Name — the key stored in your content’s front matter, used to access it in your templates. Description — a human friendly description of what the field does and/or instructions for your editors. Hidden — hides the field in the editor, but allows developers to set default values or maintain the field for legacy purposes.

item.title: Tags Field
item.description: Options General Label — the human-friendly label shown above the input field in the editor. Name — the key stored in your content’s front matter, used to access it in your templates. Description — a human friendly description of what the field does and/or instructions for your editors. Hidden — hides the field in the editor, but allows developers to set default values or maintain the field for legacy purposes.

item.title: Text Field
item.description: Options General Label — the human-friendly label shown above the input field in the editor. Name — the key stored in your content’s front matter, used to access it in your templates. Description — a human friendly description of what the field does and/or instructions for your editors. Hidden — hides the field in the editor, but allows developers to set default values or maintain the field for legacy purposes.

item.title: Textarea Field
item.description: Options General Label — the human-friendly label shown above the input field in the editor. Name — the key stored in your content’s front matter, used to access it in your templates. Description — a human friendly description of what the field does and/or instructions for your editors. Hidden — hides the field in the editor, but allows developers to set default values or maintain the field for legacy purposes.

item.title: Toggle Field
item.description: A boolean toggle. Options General Label — the human-friendly label shown above the input field in the editor. Name — the key stored in your content’s front matter, used to access it in your templates. Description — a human friendly description of what the field does and/or instructions for your editors. Hidden — hides the field in the editor, but allows developers to set default values or maintain the field for legacy purposes.
